pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh explicitly check SLVS_PATH
Task description and export_result.sh explicitly check STEP_PATH
Setup script verifies /opt/solvespace_samples/divider.slvs which is downloaded from official solvespace.com tutorial box-parts.zip

Geometrical analysis: subtracting a 12-sided prismatic 3D divider (165.1x22.86x5.99mm) from a 150x80x15mm block creates a continuous trench adding internal faces. While verifier.py is missing from the repository, the geometric result structurally exceeds 15 faces.
Geometrical analysis: the divider's 5.99mm thickness and 22.86mm width do not fully sever the 15mm thick, 80mm wide block, leaving a 9.01mm contiguous floor. The result remains 1 single solid body.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the task uses real tutorial data (box-parts.zip) from solvespace.com. The target part divider.slvs is imported to form a cavity in a 150x80x15mm block. Geometrical analysis confirms that subtracting the 5.99mm thick, 22.86mm wide divider creates a trench that leaves a 9.01mm solid floor, maintaining exactly 1 expected solid body and generating a geometry with at least 15 faces. The expected output paths for SLVS and STEP files exactly match the instructions and export scripts. Note that the specified verifier.py script is completely missing from the repository, making programmatic verification impossible.
